title: Renewable Energy for Everyone
passage:
What is Renewable Energy?

Renewable energy comes from natural sources. These sources can be used again and again. The sun, wind, and water are examples of renewable energy. They are clean and do not pollute the air. This is good for our planet.

Solar energy is from the sun. We can use solar panels to catch sunlight. This sunlight is changed into electricity. Many houses have solar panels on the roof.

Wind energy comes from the wind. Wind turbines are tall machines that turn when the wind blows. They make electricity. Wind farms are places where many wind turbines are together.

Water energy is from rivers and oceans. We can use the movement of water to make electricity. This is called hydroelectric power. It is very strong and helps many people.

Using renewable energy is important. It helps to save the Earth. It can make our air cleaner and reduce climate change. Everyone can use renewable energy in their lives.

questions.text: What is an example of renewable energy?
questions.explanation: Wind is mentioned as an example of renewable energy in the passage.

questions.explanation: The passage says that renewable energy is clean and does not pollute.

questions.text: What do we use to make electricity from wind?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that wind turbines turn to make electricity.
